title: Investigate slowness with buildah bud --layers
body: Refer to issue in libpod for details:  https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3136

comments.body: in my testing today, the margin between builds that I was able to complete was just seconds over several minutes.  As the original Dockerfile supplied in https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3136 is no longer working and we've had a number of speed improvements put into play, I'm going to close this out.  @jpic please reopen or create a new PR as appropriate if you don't agree.

Thanks for the feedback @TomSweeneyRedHat, highly appreciated. However, I now most definitively share the opinion that layer caching is vastly inferior to host controlled caching, ie. `yum install --root $(buildah mount $ctr)`, which still leverages cache for previous package when adding a new one, unlike the layer-based cache ... as such I'm preparing to migrate away from docker-compose.yml and Dockerfile in favor of a pod.yml that I'm designing and building a tool for in the [podctl](https://github.com/yourlabs/podctl) repository, I'm happy with the PoC I've setup in half a day and have a TDD rewrite currently in progress, as such, I don't plan into trying this again.

Thanks a heap for your support, have a great day !
